additional_metadata.note: Part of the dbt Global Circuit Series.

events.agenda: 17:30 Welcome; 18:30 How Rebtel increased data product value talk (30 min); 19:00 For Builders, by Builders talk (30 min); 19:00-21:30 Networking, Discussions & Drinks.
events.event_description: This meetup is designed for builders, thinkers, and tinkerers — folks who want to roll up their sleeves, learn from each other, and imagine what's next for the analytics engineering stack.
events.event_name: dbt Global Circuit Series: Stockholm dbt Meetup
events.raw_text:
Tue, Jun 10, 2025 · 5:30 PM to 9:30 PM CEST

18.30: How Rebtel increased data product value: A migration story (Abraham Setiawan, Analytics Engineer @ Rebtel)
19.00: For Builders, by Builders: The Latest Tools from dbt Labs (Ludwig Sewall, Solutions Architect @ dbt Labs)

talks.description: How Rebtel improved data quality and data team productivity by migrating from legacy architecture to dbt Cloud.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 18.30: How Rebtel increased data product value: A migration story (30 min) (Abraham Setiawan, Analytics Engineer @ Rebtel)
talks.title: How Rebtel Increased Data Product Value: A Migration Story

talks.description: A dive into the latest tools and ideas dbt Labs has been shipping, what they unlock, how they fit together, and why they were built the way they were.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 19.00: For Builders, by Builders: The Latest Tools from dbt Labs (30m) (Ludwig Sewall, Solutions Architect @ dbt Labs)
talks.title: For Builders, by Builders: The Latest Tools from dbt Labs

events.agenda: 17.30 Welcome; 18.30 Semantic layer use cases talk (30m); 19:00 Testing data products talk (30m); 19:30 Understanding SQL talk (30m); 20.00-21.30 Networking, Discussions & Drinks.
events.event_description: This dbt Meetup is an opportunity for the local Stockholm dbt Community to connect and collaborate. If you work with data, this event is for you.
events.raw_text:
Thu, Mar 20, 2025 · 5:30 PM to 8:30 PM CET

18.30: Pushing the limits – the next-level use cases for semantic layer (Johan Baltzar - Co-founder & CEO - Steep)
19:00: A strategic approach to testing Data Products (Stephen Murphy - SYNQ)
19:30: Towards a deeper understanding of SQL (Lucas Paes - Software Engineer - dbt Labs)

talks.description: Pushing the semantic layer beyond basic centrally-defined metrics into new territories like agentic AI models, drill-downs, and maps.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 18.30: Pushing the limits – the next-level use cases for semantic layer (30m) (Johan Baltzar - Co-founder & CEO - Steep)
talks.title: Pushing the Limits: The Next-Level Use Cases for Semantic Layer

talks.description: A strategic, software-engineering-inspired approach to testing data products, anchored in the 'Data Products' concept with a practical dbt implementation walkthrough.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 19:00: A strategic approach to testing Data Products (30m) (Stephen Murphy - SYNQ)
talks.title: A Strategic Approach to Testing Data Products

talks.description: A look at dbt Labs' vision for a more interoperable SQL ecosystem via compiler technology and the future of analytics engineering.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 19:30: Towards a deeper understanding of SQL (30m) (Lucas Paes - Software Engineer - dbt Labs)
talks.title: Towards a Deeper Understanding of SQL

events.agenda: Meet and Greet (30m); Keynote - Innovating with dbt (60m); TBD possible additional session (30m); Discussions with peers (60m).
events.event_description: Didn't make it to Vegas for Coalesce this year? Made it to Vegas and want to keep learning? Join the Stockholm dbt meetup for a watch party where we will stream the most relevant sessions.
events.raw_text:
Thu, Oct 24, 2024 · 5:30 PM to 9:30 PM CEST

Join the Stockholm dbt meetup for a watch party where we will stream the most relevant sessions. Agenda: Meet and Greet, Keynote - Innovating with dbt, Discussions with peers.

events.agenda: 17.30-18.00 Registration and mingle; 18.00-18.15 Welcome; 18.15-18.45 LLM dbt model generation talk; 18.45-19.15 dbt metadata automation talk; 19.15-19.45 Peer exchange/community discussion; 19.45-21.30 Network, Food & Drinks.
events.event_description: What are dbt Meetups? dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt.
events.raw_text:
Thu, May 30, 2024 · 5:30 PM to 9:30 PM CEST

18.15-18.45 Presentation: LLM assisted generation of dbt models for enterprise scale projects - Ludwig Sewall (Solita)
18.45 - 19.15 Presentation: Leveraging your dbt metadata to build powerful automation - Fernando Brito (SELECT)
19.15 - 19.45 Peer Exchange/Community Discussion moderated by Kshitij Aranke (dbt Labs) and Solita

talks.description: Ideas and hands-on strategies for using LLMs to automate migrating projects with 1000+ models from legacy ETL tools to dbt, covering migration strategies, source code parsing, and model generation.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Presentation: LLM assisted generation of dbt models for enterprise scale projects - Ludwig Sewall (Solita). Migrating projects with 1000+ models from legacy ETL tools to dbt can be a tedious and manual work.
talks.title: LLM Assisted Generation of dbt Models for Enterprise Scale Projects

talks.description: How Fernando built an open-source project to automatically connect BI tool dashboards with the dbt models they consume, exposing relationships on dbt's data lineage using dbt metadata.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Presentation: Leveraging your dbt metadata to build powerful automation - Fernando Brito (SELECT). Every dbt project exposes metadata that can be used to introspect and augment the project itself.
talks.title: Leveraging Your dbt Metadata to Build Powerful Automation

talks.description: A moderated peer exchange with three discussion groups: Embracing AI, Data Analytics at Scale, and Analytics Engineering Best Practices.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Peer Exchange/ Community Discussion: Joint table discussion organised by moderator Kshitij Aranke (dbt Labs) and Solita.
talks.title: Peer Exchange / Community Discussion

additional_metadata.note: Attendees were required to sign an NDA to gain access to the building.
